qualityGates: Issue #598 and related issue comments are read at runtime before implementation decisions.
Reuse audit records existing SDK, tasks-mux, tool-mux, hooks-mux, agent-core, agent-platform, and plugin registry surfaces before new infrastructure is introduced.
Runtime call paths are traced and later source edits stay on traced live paths unless the integration contract justifies a new seam.
Contract tests are authored before production implementation and cover task schema metadata, tasks-mux routing, tool-mux dispatch, hooks-mux lifecycle mapping, and plugin registry compatibility.
Task definition and task.json metadata changes are additive and preserve replay of historical task records.
Effect routing uses tasks-mux as the responder/backend routing authority and does not introduce a standalone SDK effect executor.
Concrete tool execution uses or adapts tool-mux ToolRegistry, ToolDispatcher, and McpBridge without silently breaking DeferredToolRegistry or McpToolRegistry consumers.
PreToolUse and PostToolUse dispatch through a hooks-mux-backed bridge on the traced tool-mux path, with documented fail-open/fail-closed behavior.
SDK runtime lifecycle hooks map to hooks-mux events without breaking existing SDK shell/plugin hook discovery.
SDK and platform plugin registry responsibilities are reconciled with compatibility coverage for installed-plugin discovery and migration behavior.
Verification commands pass, diff hygiene passes, source audits find no standalone executor or direct agent-mux bypass regression, and final code review approves.
outOfScope: Building a standalone SDK effect executor separate from tasks-mux.
Implementing unrelated BackgroundEffects or ConcurrentEffects behavior beyond compatibility preservation.
Rewriting all plugin marketplace or installation flows unrelated to registry ownership boundaries.
Requiring live MCP servers, provider credentials, network access, or installed external agents for tests.
